steps.title: Connect two solutions to the discriminant
steps.content:
The equation is quadratic in $x$, and it is given to have two real solutions. For a quadratic, two real solutions require a positive discriminant:
$$\Delta \gt 0$$
For $rx^2 - 28sx + 7r = 0$, use $a=r$, $b=-28s$, and $c=7r$:
$$(-28s)^2 - 4(r)(7r) \gt 0$$
$$784s^2 - 28r^2 \gt 0$$
$$28s^2 \gt r^2$$

steps.title: Find the minimum value of r/s
steps.content:
Divide by $s^2$; this is positive even though $s$ is negative:
$$\left(\frac{r}{s}\right)^2 \lt 28$$
So $\left|\dfrac{r}{s}\right| \lt \sqrt{28}=2\sqrt7$. Since $r\gt0$ and $s\lt0$, the ratio $r/s$ is negative, so:
$$\frac{r}{s}\gt-2\sqrt7$$
The minimum possible value of $k$ is $-2\sqrt7 \approx -5.29$.
